title: Japanese Grilled Lamb Skewers
description: Delicious lamb skewers marinated in a mixture of Japanese flavors, then grilled to perfection. Serve as a main dish or as an appetizer.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: Cut into 1-inch cubes for even grilling.

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality soy sauce for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: mirin
ingredients.notes: A sweet Japanese cooking wine that adds depth to the marinade.

ingredients.name: sugar
ingredients.notes: A small amount of sugar helps balance the acidity in the marinade.

ingredients.name: green onions, chopped
ingredients.notes: Add some chopped green onions on top for garnish.

ingredients.name: sesame oil
ingredients.notes: Use sesame oil for a nutty flavor and aroma.

ingredients.name: silky sake
ingredients.notes: Add a splash of silky sake for added depth to the marinade.

ingredients.name: skewers
ingredients.notes: Use metal or bamboo skewers for grilling.

ingredients.name: dried seaweed
ingredients.notes: Add some dried seaweed on top for added texture and flavor.

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, mirin, sugar, and sesame oil.
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and toss to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto the skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the grill with a little oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the lamb skewers for 8-10 minutes per side, or until cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Transfer the skewers to a plate and garnish with chopped green onions and dried seaweed.
Serve hot with your favorite sides.
